title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski hit a walk-off single for the Braves in the 10th inning, defeating the Red Sox 3-2.
Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed two solo home runs over five innings, striking out six without walking a batter.
Boston's Marcelo Mayer tied the game with a solo home run in the seventh, his second of the season.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Stefon Diggs is interested in rejoining the Patriots, but it's unlikely due to a pending trade for A.J. Brown.
A.J. Brown's $32 million annual contract creates financial constraints, impacting the Patriots' pursuit of Stefon Diggs.
Stefon Diggs recorded 1,045 yards with the Patriots last year; his return would complicate the receiver lineup.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, 15, was last seen Tuesday afternoon near 965 Blue Hill Ave in Dorchester.
He is described as a Black male, 5'8", thin build, brown eyes, black hair, often visits Columbia Road and Harambee Park.
Police are investigating and urge anyone with information to contact District B-3 detectives or submit tips anonymously.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, allegedly shot his girlfriend's mother and another man on Mother's Day in Dorchester.
The incident occurred at 69 Kingsdale St. with the female victim hospitalized with life-threatening injuries.
Broomes faces charges including assault with intent to murder and is held without bail pending a dangerousness hearing.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Boston Red Sox's former players like Chris Sale and Nathan Eovaldi perform better than the current 2026 team's roster.
The theoretical lineup includes former key players such as Mookie Betts, Xander Bogaerts, and Kyle Schwarber.
Schwarber leads MLB with 18 home runs, while Dominic Smith emerges as a breakout star with a .353 average.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roman Anthony has been on the 10-day injured list since May 7 due to a wrist sprain.
Anthony has removed his wrist brace and started light activities, but has not resumed swinging a bat.
Red Sox manager Chad Tracy is optimistic Anthony will begin batting practice soon pending recovery progress.

title: Jeep drives into Norwood day care
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Jeep crashed into BrightPath daycare in Norwood; no injuries were reported.
The driver accidentally accelerated while dropping off a passenger, pushing the Jeep into the building.
BrightPath daycare was temporarily closed due to moderate structural damage pending further inspection.

title: Here’s what experts are predicting the Patriots’ 2026 record will be
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict the New England Patriots will achieve a 10-7 record for the 2026 season.
Patriots face a challenging schedule including games against the Seahawks, Chargers, and Chiefs.
The Patriots made key offseason additions but may still finish with a lower win count due to tougher competition.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Marty, Life is Short' documentary on Martin Short is now streaming on Netflix, featuring Steve Martin and Eugene Levy.
'Mars Attacks!' is available for free streaming on YouTube and Plex, starring Martin Short in a sci-fi comedy directed by Tim Burton.
'Pop Culture Jeopardy!' Season 2 hosted by Colin Jost has moved to Netflix, now featuring duo teams instead of trios.

title: Mayor Wu remains firm as councilors consider rejecting her budget
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston Mayor Michelle Wu proposed a $4.9 billion budget, marking the smallest spending increase since the financial crisis.
Several City Council members consider rejecting Wu's budget, urging increased spending and fewer cuts by June 10 deadline.
Wu warns that any resubmitted budget could further decrease spending; City Council lacks power to raise overall budget amount.
